title: cordiera concolor var. goyana
intro: Cordiera concolor var. goyana, also known as "Yerba de la montaña" or "Mountain tea," is a plant species native to the Amazon rainforest in South America, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. Traditionally, this plant has been used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits associated with Cordiera concolor var. goyana include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and supporting the immune system. The plant is often prepared as an herbal tea by infusing the leaves in hot water, and it can also be found in capsule or tincture form for those who prefer a more concentrated dosage.
